message:
feat(abi): rewrite fopen/fdopen to be native-only with NativeFile (bd-9chy.5)

- Remove host delegation path from fopen() - no longer calls
  prefer_host_stdio_streams() or host_fopen_fn()
- Remove host delegation path from fdopen() - no longer calls
  host_fdopen_fn()
- Add fdopen_native_impl() helper that creates NativeFile, determines
  buffering mode via raw isatty check, and registers in native registry
- Add raw_isatty() using TIOCGWINSZ ioctl syscall (no libc dependency)
- fopen now: parses mode, opens via SYS_openat, creates NativeFile with
  proper flags, sets vtable to NATIVE_IO_JUMP_T, registers in
  native_stream_registry, returns actual *mut NativeFile pointer
- fdopen now: validates fd/mode, creates NativeFile via fdopen_native_impl
- fopen64 remains an alias to fopen (already correct on 64-bit)
- Leaves prefer_host_stdio_streams/host_fopen_fn/host_fdopen_fn unused
  (will be removed in bd-9chy.11)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-7ef9","title":"bd-h5x subtask: Callthrough census + decommission sequencing by symbol family","description":"Background:
- Callthrough elimination work needs symbol-family-level truth to avoid hidden dependence on host glibc.

Goal:
- Produce current callthrough census and decommission plan by family/symbol with risk and sequence.

Deliverables:
1) Enumerate all remaining callthrough symbols and their owning modules.
2) Rank by runtime impact and replacement complexity.
3) Define migration order with rollback strategy.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Census is machine-readable and tied to support matrix.
- Decommission plan is dependency-aware and implementation-ready.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for census extraction/parsing helpers.
- Structured logs for census generation and ranking decisions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberStone","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:32.598635951Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T08:51:07.423915839Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T08:51:07.423897114Z","close_reason":"Completed support-matrix-tied callthrough census artifact with dependency-aware decommission waves, generator/check scripts, structured log/report emission, and passing gate/verification evidence.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["callthrough","critique","docs","syscall","verification"],"comments":[{"id":1062,"issue_id":"bd-7ef9","author":"AmberStone","text":"Implemented deterministic callthrough census + dependency-aware decommission sequencing tied to support_matrix.json.\n\nArtifacts:\n- tests/conformance/callthrough_census.v1.json\n- scripts/generate_callthrough_census.py\n- scripts/check_callthrough_census.sh\n- crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/callthrough_census_test.rs\n- target/conformance/callthrough_census.report.json (generated by gate)\n- target/conformance/callthrough_census.log.jsonl (generated by gate)\n\nValidation:\n- python3 scripts/generate_callthrough_census.py --support-matrix support_matrix.json --output tests/conformance/callthrough_census.v1.json\n- scripts/check_callthrough_census.sh\n- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/cargo-target-amber cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test callthrough_census_test --no-run\n- /tmp/cargo-target-amber/debug/deps/callthrough_census_test-13fe70897b726eac\n- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/cargo-target-amber cargo run -p frankenlibc-harness --bin harness -- verify --fixture tests/conformance/fixtures --report /tmp/frankenlibc_strict_hardened_verify_bd_7ef9.md\n\nNotes:\n- Artifact captures support-matrix summary drift explicitly (: summary reports 54 vs 49 derived callthrough symbols).\n- Direct

+{"id":"bd-9chy.15","title":"stdio: build NATIVE_IO_JUMP_T vtable + 17 trampolines for indirect dispatch","description":"## Background

Plan v4 §10. Many glibc-built binaries (especially with -fno-plt) call vfprintf indirectly through ((struct _IO_FILE_plus *)fp)->vtable->__xsputn(...) rather than through the PLT-resolved vfprintf symbol. If our vtable pointer is null or wrong, the program crashes inside __xsputn.

## Depends on

bd-9chy.1 (NativeFile must already have a vtable slot)

## What to do

1. In crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/io_internal_abi.rs, define repr(C) struct _IO_jump_t with the 17 standard slots: __dummy, __dummy2, __finish, __overflow, __underflow, __uflow, __pbackfail, __xsputn, __xsgetn, __seekoff, __seekpos, __setbuf, __sync, __doallocate, __read, __write, __seek, __close, __stat, __showmanyc, __imbue. Field offsets pinned to glibc 2.34.
2. Create a static NATIVE_IO_JUMP_T: _IO_jump_t = ... initialized with extern "C" function pointers to Rust trampolines.
3. Each trampoline takes a *mut FILE, casts to *mut NativeFile, acquires the recursive lock, and dispatches to the native kernel function used by the public ABI entrypoint. The trampolines for __xsputn and __xsgetn are the high-volume ones; benchmark them.
4. Every NativeFile constructor (bd-9chy.5..10) sets `self.vtable = &NATIVE_IO_JUMP_T as *const _ as *mut _`.
5. Trampoline must handle the foreign-pointer-adoption case: if the FILE* it receives has the bloom precheck saying "not ours," route through adopt_foreign_file (separate bead bd-9chy.16). For now, panic with a clear message; the next bead wires real adoption.
6. Unit test: build a small Rust test that takes &mut NATIVE_IO_JUMP_T.__xsputn, calls it with a NativeFile* and verifies bytes are written.

## Done

- _IO_jump_t struct defined with offsets matching glibc
- NATIVE_IO_JUMP_T static built and wired into NativeFile constructors
- All 17 trampolines have implementations (some can be no-ops or default behavior)
- Unit test for indirect __xsputn dispatch passes
- Snapshot test tests/runtime_math/golden/io_jump_dispatch.v1.json captures vtable-vs-PLT call counts on a representative workload","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:30:03.906540102Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-13T17:38:04.026649614Z","closed_at":"2026-04-13T17:38:04.026388970Z","close_reason":"Implemented _IO_jump_t vtable with 21 trampolines for indirect dispatch. All slots wired to native stdio functions. NativeFile::new() sets vtable to NATIVE_IO_JUMP_T. Layout tests added. Also fixed TLS corruption and type mismatches.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["stdio","vtable"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.15","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:30:03.906540102Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.15","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:30:03.906540102Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-9chy.2","title":"support_matrix: tighten classifier to inspect ABI function bodies and add WrapsHostLibc state","description":"## Background

Reality-check found that tests/conformance/support_matrix_maintenance_report.v1.json was regenerated 2026-04-08 with zero deltas vs the 2026-02-18 baseline, despite ongoing March/April commits to ABI files including stdio. The current classifier in scripts/generate_support_matrix_maintenance.py (and the Rust harness equivalent in crates/frankenlibc-harness if it exists) inspects only signatures, not bodies. As a result, ABI functions that are labelled Implemented but in fact call libc::fwrite or host_*_fn() at runtime are not flagged.

This bead is the body-inspecting classifier and the new explicit WrapsHostLibc taxonomy state. It runs *first* in the epic order because every other bead depends on having a mechanical way to verify "did I close the gap I think I closed."

## What to do

1. Add a new variant WrapsHostLibc to the support taxonomy (alongside Implemented, RawSyscall, GlibcCallThrough, Stub). Update the JSON schema, the Rust enum if any, and the README/AGENTS.md taxonomy descriptions.
2. Extend scripts/generate_support_matrix_maintenance.py to walk every *_abi.rs file in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/ via syn (or tree-sitter, or naive ripgrep — the rule is structural).
3. For each public extern "C" fn, check the body for: any libc::f* call (fopen/fclose/fread/fwrite/fseek/ftell/fflush/setvbuf/ungetc/feof/ferror/clearerr/fileno/getline/getdelim/printf), any libc::malloc/free/realloc, any host_*_fn() invocation, any crate::host_resolve::resolve_host_symbol_* call. If any match, classify as WrapsHostLibc.
4. Regenerate support_matrix.json. EXPECT a one-time bump: stdio symbols + rpc/glibc_internal symbols flip from Implemented to WrapsHostLibc. This is the corrective action — the count change is the audit telling the truth for the first time.
5. Add the L0 closure gate clause in tests/conformance/closure_contract.v1.json: WrapsHostLibc must be 0 for L0 closure.
6. Run tests/conformance/check_support_matrix_maintenance.sh and confirm it now produces a non-trivial delta vs the prior baseline.

## Done

- New WrapsHostLibc state exists in schema and code
- Classifier inspects function bodies, not signatures
- support_matrix.json regenerates with the corrective bump
- closure_contract.v1.json gates on WrapsHostLibc == 0
- CI fails on a freshly added libc::fwrite in any *_abi.rs file (verify with a one-line test PR)
- README and AGENTS.md taxonomy descriptions updated to mention WrapsHostLibc","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:26:27.159467007Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T18:02:46.438166117Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["classifier","foundation","stdio"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.2","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:26:27.159467007Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Deliverables:

Formally blocked by bd-gn9 but implementation already follows the milli-units convention. Ready to close once bd-gn9 resolves.","created_at":"2026-02-10T17:20:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-9dq5","title":"Track D: Implement gai_cancel/gai_error/gai_suspend as synchronous-only stubs with proper error returns","description":"## Goal
Replace 3 async DNS stub functions in unistd_abi.rs (lines 17404-17423) that incorrectly return EAI_SYSTEM/ENOSYS with correct POSIX error returns.

## Critical Design Insight
FrankenLibC does NOT export getaddrinfo_a() — the function that creates async DNS requests. Therefore, gai_error/gai_cancel/gai_suspend will NEVER receive valid in-flight request handles. The correct implementation is NOT a thread pool — it is simply returning the correct error code for 'no such request' or 'no pending requests'.

## Current State (WRONG)
All 3 functions set errno=ENOSYS and return EAI_SYSTEM. This is incorrect because:
- gai_cancel should return EAI_NOCANCEL (not a valid request) or EAI_ALLDONE
- gai_error should return EAI_SYSTEM with errno=EINVAL (invalid gaicb)
- gai_suspend with an empty/null list should return EAI_ALLDONE (no pending requests)

Programs that probe async DNS availability by calling gai_suspend(NULL, 0, NULL) get EAI_SYSTEM instead of EAI_ALLDONE, which may cause them to disable async features unnecessarily.

## Correct Implementation (SIMPLE)
### gai_cancel(req)
- If req is NULL: return EAI_NOCANCEL
- Otherwise: return EAI_ALLDONE (no async requests exist, so it is already 'done')

### gai_error(req)
- If req is NULL: set errno=EINVAL, return EAI_SYSTEM
- Otherwise: return 0 (treat any gaicb as 'completed' since we resolve synchronously)

### gai_suspend(list, nitems, timeout)
- If list is NULL or nitems <= 0: return 0 (nothing to wait for)
- Scan list entries: if all non-NULL entries have ar_result set, return 0
- If timeout is NULL: return EAI_AGAIN (would block forever waiting for nothing)
- If timeout is not NULL: return EAI_AGAIN (timeout immediately since no async work pending)

## Why This Is Correct
The POSIX spec says gai_suspend waits for 'at least one request to complete.' Since FrankenLibC resolves all getaddrinfo calls synchronously, any gaicb passed to gai_suspend has ALREADY been resolved by the time gai_suspend is called. The correct behavior is to check if the requests are done and return immediately.

## Testing
- Unit test: gai_cancel(NULL) returns EAI_NOCANCEL
- Unit test: gai_cancel(non_null_ptr) returns EAI_ALLDONE
- Unit test: gai_error(NULL) returns EAI_SYSTEM with errno=EINVAL
- Unit test: gai_suspend(NULL, 0, NULL) returns 0
- Unit test: gai_suspend(empty_list, 0, NULL) returns 0
- e2e: scripts/check_gai_async_stubs.sh verifying correct return codes via small C test

## Structured Logging
All 3 functions must emit structured trace on invocation:
  trace_id, mode, api_family=resolv, symbol=gai_cancel|gai_error|gai_suspend, decision_path, errno, latency_ns

## Acceptance Criteria
All 3 functions return correct POSIX error codes. No ENOSYS returns. Tests pass. Clippy clean. e2e script passes.

## Note
If getaddrinfo_a() is ever implemented in the future, these functions would need to be upgraded to poll a real request registry. But that is a separate epic (P4+) since async DNS is extremely rare.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:19:24.489961531Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:46:07.802361147Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:46:07.802082449Z","close_reason":"Replaced incorrect ENOSYS returns with correct POSIX error codes. gai_cancel: NULL->EAI_NOTCANCELED, non-null->EAI_ALLDONE (all sync, nothing to cancel). gai_error: NULL->EAI_SYSTEM/EINVAL, non-null->0 (synchronous resolver means complete). gai_suspend: nitems<=0->0, otherwise->0 (all requests already resolved synchronously). No thread pool needed since getaddrinfo_a is not exported. Clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["async","resolv","stub-elimination"],"comments":[{"id":1364,"issue_id":"bd-9dq5","author":"ubuntu","text":"Risk: gai_* async DNS used by almost no programs. ENOSYS acceptable for 99%+ workloads. If too complex, fallback: implement gai_error as immediate-return (EAI_ALLDONE for completed, EAI_NOCANCEL for unknown) and gai_suspend as no-wait poll. This synchronous stub passes most programs checking async DNS availability.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:21:02Z"}]}
